---
title: F. TLS + credentials
---

The web UI is served over HTTPS and asks you to sign in. This step creates the
two things that makes possible: a **TLS certificate** so the browser can open a
secure connection, and a **login account** so you can authenticate.

::: {.callout-important}
## Requires: Configure (step D)
Both commands read settings from the `.env` and `config.yaml` you set up in
[D. Configure](configure.qmd) — the certificate paths, the server name, and the
password salt. Complete step D first.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows: use Git Bash
All `make` commands must be run in **Git Bash**, not PowerShell or cmd.exe.
:::

## 1. Generate the TLS certificate

```bash
make ssl
```

This creates a self-signed certificate and private key and writes them into
your certificates directory (`PIPELINE_SSL_DIR`, default `./data/certs`):

| File | `.env` variable | Default name |
|---|---|---|
| Certificate | `PIPELINE_SSL_CERT` | `server.crt` |
| Private key | `PIPELINE_SSL_KEY` | `server.key` |

The certificate is issued for the host name in `PIPELINE_SERVERNAME` (default
`localhost`) and is valid for one year.

## 2. Create your login

```bash
make gen-credentials
```

This prompts for a password and creates the account **`admin`**. It writes a
hashed credentials file to `credentials.json` in the repository root (the path
in `PIPELINE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`, default `./credentials.json`) — your password is
never stored in plain text.

To set the password without an interactive prompt (for example in a script),
pass `PASSWORD=`:

```bash
make gen-credentials PASSWORD=your-password-here
```

::: {.callout-important}
## The salt must already be set — and never change afterwards
`make gen-credentials` mixes your password with the `salt` from `config.yaml`
before hashing. If you change that salt **after** generating credentials, every
stored password becomes invalid and you'll be locked out. Rotate the salt once
in [D. Configure](configure.qmd), then leave it alone.
:::

## If something goes wrong

- **`doctor` says the cert or key is missing.** Re-run `make ssl` and check that
  `PIPELINE_SSL_DIR`, `PIPELINE_SSL_CERT`, and `PIPELINE_SSL_KEY` in `.env` match
  the files that were created.
- **`doctor` says the credentials file is missing.** Re-run
  `make gen-credentials`. The file is written to the repo root by default; make
  sure `PIPELINE_CREDENTIALS_FILE` in `.env` points at it.
- **You can't log in even though the files exist.** The password was almost
  certainly hashed with a different salt than the app is using. Set the salt in
  `config.yaml` first, then re-run `make gen-credentials` so the hash matches.
